data.body:
TARGETS — VERIFICATION CORRECTIONS (Explore audit). Script map ALL CORRECT. Findings:
* ticket-fsck.sh prints findings INLINE (CORRUPT:/MISSING_CREATE:/WARN:/PUSH_PENDING: ...). --output json requires refactoring it to ACCUMULATE findings into an array then emit once at the end (data is all there; it's a structure change, not new data).
* ticket-bridge-fsck.py ALREADY builds {orphaned[],duplicates[],stale[]} via audit_bridge_mappings() — just add --output json to emit the dict instead of _format_report() text. Easy.
* list-epics exit codes to PRESERVE: 0=unblocked epics exist, 1=no epics, 2=all blocked (ticket-list-epics.sh ~:394-405). Also fsck exit 0/1 preserve.
* READY shape RESOLVED: ready --output json = JSON ARRAY of full TicketState (same element as list/search), NOT id-list — matches current --json behavior. (ready's --output handled in T2; T4 just confirms shape.)
* LIBRARY/MCP GAPS — no rebar.summary(), no rebar.list_epics(), no bridge-fsck lib/MCP wrapper. check_ac/quality_check return {passed,output} today. SCOPE DECISION NEEDED (see story-level question): whether to add lib/MCP parity wrappers + whether check_ac/quality_check switch to the structured shape (breaking) or stay {passed,output}.
* Dispatcher usage strings (create/list-epics/summary/check-ac/quality-check/fsck/bridge-fsck) need --output documented.
